title: athamanta lumpeanum
intro: Athamanta lumpeanum, also known as Sea Holly or Carline Thistle, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other compounds. The health benefits of Athamanta lumpeanum include its potential to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, as well as its ability to support the immune system and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Athamanta lumpeanum is often used as a tincture or infusion, and it is also used in the production of herbal teas, salves, and creams to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
